channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: New Mac for Christmas? Try these 6 apps and games with your new Apple computer
item.description: Did your Christmas morning start off with a Mac under the tree? No matter if you unwrapped a new MacBook, iMac, or Mac mini, that hardware is just the beginning of a gift that will keep on giving. In 2025, there are more apps and games for Apple computers than ever.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: The ‘internet of beings’ is the next fronteir that could change humanity and healthcare
item.description: In the 1966 film Fantastic Voyage, a spacecraft and its crew are shrunk to microscopic size and injected into the body of an injured astronaut to remove a life-threatening blood clot from his brain.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: The 34 best shows of 2025: TechRadar's top TV picks on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more
item.description: 2025 has been jam-packed with top-tier TV shows – here are TechRadar's favorite ones of the last 12 months.
item.dc:creator: Tom Power, TechRadar

item.title: DoorDash launches Zesty, an AI app for finding local food
item.description: DoorDash launches Zesty, an AI-powered social app that recommends restaurants through conversational search, now testing in San Francisco and New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: True Detective season 5: everything we know so far about the hit HBO Max show's return
item.description: True Detective season 5 is officially on the way – here's everything we know about the hit HBO Max show's return.
item.dc:creator: Grace Dean, TechRadar

item.title: High severity flaw in MongoDB could allow memory leakage
item.description: Document database vendor MongoDB has advised customers to update immediately following the discovery of a flaw that could allow unauthenticated users to read uninitialized heap memory.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Acquisition more than IT drove the news in 2025
item.description: Federal IT and acquisition experts say the FedRAMP update and artificial intelligence pilots moving to production were among the big story lines last year. The post Acquisition more than IT drove the news in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple's iPad in 2025: the highs, lows, and what we want to see in 2026
item.description: Apple’s 2025 iPad lineup wasn’t about flashy redesigns, but meaningful performance upgrades and the arrival of iPadOS 26 combined to deliver the most complete iPad experience yet.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Krol, TechRadar

item.title: Most parked domains now push scams and malware
item.description: Cybersecurity firm Infoblox reveals that over 90 percent of parked domains now redirect visitors to scams and malware, making simple typos extremely dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 10 Nvidia stories of 2025 – From data center to AI factory
item.description: 2025 was not just about faster GPUs. It was about the fundamental re-architecture of the enterprise data center from a storage/retrieval hub to a manufacturing plant for intelligence – or the AI factory.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to watch the Death in Paradise 2025 Christmas Special online from anywhere
item.description: When four friends wake after Christmas, a shocking murder changes everything. Here's how to watch the Death in Paradise 2025 Christmas special online from anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Krishi Chowdhary, TechRadar

item.title: Larry Ellison, not Elon Musk, was the tech titan who defined 2025
item.description: Oracle has embarked on a historic build-out of data centers optimized for AI that’s proceeding faster than some expected.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: The Switch 2 has had a brilliant first year, but cracks in performance are already starting to show
item.description: Nintendo’s year was dominated by Switch 2 speculation and the arrival of the system, but there’s plenty more that happened, too.
item.dc:creator: Lloyd Coombes, TechRadar

item.title: DoD expands login options beyond CAC
item.description: The memo does not include any requirements for authenticators to be phishing-resistant, even though DoD is required to retire phishable authenticators by 2027. The post DoD expands login options beyond CAC first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Cuatro recomendaciones para que los CIO salven la brecha de confianza en la IA
item.description: Por tercer año consecutivo, el Thinkers360 AI Trust Index ha tomado el pulso a la opinión pública sobre la IA y, una vez más, sus resultados recuerdan a los CIO y CXO que la curva de innovación tecnológica sigue avanzando más rápido que la estructura ética y de gobernanza necesaria para sostenerla.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Acemagic just launched one of the weirdest-looking Ryzen AI Max+ 395 mini PCs to date - Tank M1A Pro+ supports 24TB storage, quad 8K screens and claims to match the RTX 4070M laptop GPU
item.description: Acemagic’s Tank M1A Pro+ joins the Ryzen AI Max+ 395 mini PC wave with support for four 8K screens, 128GB memory and up to 24TB SSD storage.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Williams, TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone scam tricks owners into giving phones away
item.description: New iPhone replacement scam uses pressure tactics and fake carrier calls to steal devices from buyers. Criminals claim shipping errors and demand urgent returns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Apple and Google want your ID
item.description: Apple and Google would like to see your identification, please. With the former’s “Digital ID” launch last week, both companies now let you scan a digital version of your passport at more than 250 Transportation Security Administration checkpoints, using an iPhone or Android phone.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: EU warns of possible action after the US bars 5 Europeans accused of censorship
item.description: France, Germany, the European Union and the United Kingdom are hitting out at a U.S. decision to impose travel bans on five Europeans the Trump administration accuses of pressuring tech firms to censor or suppress American views.
item.dc:creator: Lorne Cook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 5 stages to observability maturity
item.description: When CIOs talk about observability, they may refer to log dashboards, real-time causal graphs, or AI agents that surface business risk before customers feel an impact. The term has become so overloaded that even seasoned analysts sometimes flinch when they hear it.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI power tools: 6 ways to supercharge your terminal
item.description: The command line has always been the bedrock of the developer’s world. Since time immemorial, the CLI was a static place defined by the REPL (read-evaluate-print-loop). But now modern AI tools are changing that.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why Waymo went down in San Francisco
item.description: Last weekend, a gnarly power outage in San Francisco took out a number of traffic lights, which, in turn, sent a number of self-driving Waymo robotaxis into a sort of fugue state.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Heilweil, Fast Company

item.title: Consuming news from AI shifts our opinions and reality. Here’s how
item.description: Meta’s decision to end its professional fact-checking program sparked a wave of criticism in the tech and media world.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Rust vision group seeks enumeration of language design goals
item.description: To help the Rust language continue scaling across domains and usage levels, the Rust Vision Doc group recommends enumerating the design goals for evolving the language while also improving the crates package system.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Army bucks trend, to move forward with $50B MAPS contract
item.description: Despite several agencies pausing or canceling large multiple award contracts, the Army decided to proceed with its MAPS vehicle for professional services. The post Army bucks trend, to move forward with $50B MAPS contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: This AI startup is extending an olive branch between humans and machines
item.description: In the fall of 2024, six college students joined forces to start an AI company together. Five of them had met while studying computer science, computer engineering, and electrical engineering at Georgia Tech in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company
